prompt: British school class photo, late 1940s, overcast day

modifiers.effect: early colour-film rendering: muted saturation ceiling, slightly warm cast

global.rationale: This is a real monochrome photograph with no colour ground truth; the era prior governs every choice.

regions.grounding_phrase: the stone wall behind the children
regions.rationale: British institutional buildings of the period are weathered grey-brown stone.

regions.grounding_phrase: the paved ground the boys are sitting on
regions.rationale: Schoolyard asphalt/flagstone under overcast light is a cool neutral grey.

regions.grounding_phrase: the dark knitted jumper of the boy sitting cross-legged at the front left

modifiers.effect: home-dyed wool: drab greens, browns, maroons
practical dark colours that hide wear

regions.rationale: Luminance fully under-determines this; 1940s British boys' knitwear skews drab brown/maroon wool. Wide tolerance, low confidence — exactly the case the era prior exists for.

regions.grounding_phrase: the light dress of the girl standing at the far right

modifiers.effect: pale cotton prints: cream, dusty pink, faded florals

regions.rationale: Light-toned girls' dresses of the period are pale cotton, often dusty pink or cream.
